questions.question: A router has the following routes in its routing table: 10.0.0.0/8 via EIGRP, 10.1.0.0/16 via OSPF, and 10.1.1.0/24 via RIP. A packet arrives destined for 10.1.1.50. Which route will the router use?
options: 10.0.0.0/8 via EIGRP because EIGRP has the lowest AD
10.1.0.0/16 via OSPF because OSPF is a link-state protocol
10.1.1.0/24 via RIP because it is the longest prefix match
The router will load-balance across all three routes
questions.explanation: Routers always prefer the longest prefix match regardless of administrative distance or metric. The /24 route is the most specific match for 10.1.1.50, so it is selected even though RIP has the highest AD of the three protocols.

questions.question: What is the default administrative distance of OSPF?
questions.explanation: OSPF has a default administrative distance of 110. EIGRP internal is 90, and RIP is 120. AD is used to rank the trustworthiness of different routing sources.

questions.question: An engineer configures the command 'ip route 10.10.0.0 255.255.0.0 192.168.1.2 115' on a router. What does the value 115 represent?
options: The metric for the static route
The administrative distance assigned to this static route
The VLAN number for the route
questions.explanation: The trailing number on an ip route command sets a custom administrative distance. The default AD for a static route is 1. Setting it to 115 makes it a floating static route that is only used when routes with lower AD (such as OSPF at 110) are unavailable.

questions.question: Which command configures a default route on a Cisco router pointing to next-hop 203.0.113.1?
questions.explanation: A default route uses the destination 0.0.0.0 with a mask of 0.0.0.0, which matches any destination not explicitly found in the routing table. This route becomes the gateway of last resort.

questions.question: In the output of 'show ip route', what do the codes 'C' and 'L' represent?
options: C = cached route, L = learned route
C = connected network, L = local host route for the router's own interface IP
C = classful route, L = longest-match route
C = configured route, L = loopback route
questions.explanation: C indicates a connected route representing the network on a directly attached interface. L indicates a local route, which is a /32 host route for the IP address assigned to the router's own interface. Both are automatically created when an interface is configured and enabled.
